statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{10}{b-1}-8}}{{\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-7}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{10}{b-1}-8} and {\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-7}}, which is {\left(b-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(b-7\right)}.
Since b-1 and b-7 were canceled, b \ne 1 and b \ne 7 are in the lost domain condition.
